Motion To Compel Arbitration And Dismiss Action Or Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Aug-10-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND DISMISS ACTION OR STAY PROCEEDINGS

Appearances via Zoom: Kayla Lucia of Jackson Lewis (408.579.0404) for defendants and moving parties; No other appearances.

This matter is not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's motion to compel arbitration of the individual PAGA claim is granted. The court stays the representative PAGA claim pending arbitration. (Adolph v. Uber Technologies, Inc. (Cal., July 17, 2023, No. S274671) 2023 WL 4553702; Viking River Cruises, Inc. v. Moriana (2022) 142 S. Ct. 1906.)

The parties' arbitration agreement provides:

In the event it is determined that this waiver is unenforceable with respect to any claim, then this waiver shall not apply to that claim, that claim must be filed in a court of competent jurisdiction, and such court shall be the exclusive forum for that claim. If any provision, or any portion of any provision, of this Agreement is found to be invalid, void, or unenforceable, it shall be interpreted in a manner or modified to make it enforceable. If that is not possible, it shall be severed and the remaining provisions of this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.

(Loeffler Decl., pars. 15-16, Ex. D.)

This language indicates that if the class/representative waiver is unenforceable, then the class/representative claim shall remain in court. (Nickson v. Shemran, Inc. (2023) 90 Cal.App.5th 121 [compelling arbitration of individual PAGA claim in light of the severability clause].) "Doubts as to whether an arbitration [provision] applies to a particular dispute are to be resolved in favor of sending the parties to arbitration." (Vianna v. Doctors' Management Co. (1994) 27 Cal.App.4th 1186, 1189.)

This case is distinguishable from the "poison pill" cases where the unenforceable class waiver rendered the entire agreement unenforceable. (See Duran v. EmployBridge Holding Co. (2023) 92 Cal.App.5th 59 [arbitration agreement's carve-out provision--"claims under PAGA ... are not arbitrable under this Agreement"--excluded all PAGA claims from arbitration]; Westmoreland v. Kindercare Educ. LLC (2023) 90 Cal.App.5th 967 [arbitration agreement's impermissible waiver of employee's PAGA claims invalidated entire agreement under its unambiguous savings clause ("if the Waiver of Class and Collective Claims is found to be unenforceable, then this agreement is invalid and any claim brought on a class, collective, or representative action basis must be filed in a court of competent jurisdiction, and such court shall be the exclusive forum for such claims.")].)

Order signed. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (302/RBU)
